Notes: End of Anecdotes about Other Groups subsection. This subsection in the print catalogue contains a number of regional/ethnic stereotype joke types (typically Schwänke targeting specific national or religious groups). These are sparsely represented here; the catalogue numbers exist in Uther 2004 but reflect historical material that is often offensive by modern standards. Researchers needing complete coverage should consult FFC 284-286 directly with appropriate contextual framing.

Title: Cumulative Tales (parent type)
Exemplar_Tales: Parent type covering cumulative tales broadly
Notes: Cumulative tale is a chain-tale where each episode adds to the previous; see lettered subtypes and 2010-2075 range.
Gap between cumulative-tales parent type (2000) and first enumerated subtype (2010).

Title: Ehod Mi Yodea (Who Knows One?)
Exemplar_Tales: "Ehad Mi Yodea" / "Who Knows One?" (Passover Haggadah, Hebrew counting-song, medieval Ashkenazi); "The Twelve Days of Christmas" (English carol, structural parallel, 1780 first printed)
Notes: Counting cumulative tale; each verse adds a numbered item from 1 to 12 or 13.
Gap between 2010 counting-song subtypes and 2015 cluster.
